title: rubus protractus
intro: Rubus protractus, also known as the thimbleberry or Himalayan raspberry, is a type of flowering shrub native to the Himalayan region. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, particularly vitamin C, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus protractus include its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of certain diseases and promote overall well-being. Rubus protractus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, improve digestive health, and provide relief from minor aches and pains.
